body:
The Scottish Parliament may by resolution (a “Scottish rate resolution”) set the Scottish basic rate, and any other rates, for the purposes of section 11A of the Income Tax Act 2007 (which provides for the income of Scottish taxpayers which is charged at those rates).
Where a Scottish rate resolution sets more than one rate it must also set limits or make other provision to enable it to be ascertained, for the purposes of that section, which rates apply in relation to a Scottish taxpayer.
If income tax is charged at Scottish rates on the non-savings income of a Scottish taxpayer for a tax year (within the meaning of section 11A of the Income Tax Act 2007), those rates are treated for income tax purposes as if they were—
Scottish rates for all non-savings income other than property income which are set for the tax year at the same rates as the Scottish rates, and
separate Scottish rates for property income which are set for the tax year at the same rates as the Scottish rates,
but, subject to that, a Scottish rate resolution may not provide for different rates to apply in relation to different types of income.
In this Chapter a “Scottish rate” means a rate set by a Scottish rate resolution.
A Scottish rate resolution applies—
for only one tax year, and
for the whole of that year.
. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .
A Scottish rate must be a whole number or half a whole number , or zero.
A Scottish rate resolution—
must specify the tax year for which it applies,
must be made before the start of that tax year, and
must not be made more than 12 months before the start of that year.
If a Scottish rate resolution is cancelled before the start of the tax year for which it is to apply—
the Income Tax Acts have effect for that year as if the resolution had never been passed, and
the resolution may be replaced by another Scottish rate resolution.
Standing orders must provide that only a member of the Scottish Government may move a motion for a Scottish rate resolution.
heading: Section 80C

body:
Income tax is charged at Scottish rates on the non-savings income of a Scottish taxpayer.
For the purposes of this section, “non-savings income” means income which is not savings income.
This section is subject to—
section 13 (income charged at the dividend ordinary, upper and additional rates: individuals), and
any provisions of the Income Tax Acts (apart from section 10) which provide for income of an individual to be charged at different rates of income tax in some circumstances.
Section 16 has effect for determining which part of a Scottish taxpayer’s income consists of savings income.
heading: Section 11A

body:
Section 80C of the Scotland Act 1998 (power to set a Scottish rate for Scottish taxpayers) is amended as follows.
In subsection (1)
 (power to set Scottish rate) for the words from “the Scottish rate” to the end substitute “
 the Scottish basic rate, and any other rates, for the purposes of section 11A of the Income Tax Act 2007 (which provides for the income of Scottish taxpayers which is charged at those rates)”.
For subsection (2) substitute—
Where a Scottish rate resolution sets more than one rate it must also set limits or make other provision to enable it to be ascertained, for the purposes of that section, which rates apply in relation to a Scottish taxpayer.
But a Scottish rate resolution may not provide for different rates to apply in relation to different types of income.
In this Chapter a “Scottish rate” means a rate set by a Scottish rate resolution.
Omit subsection (4).
In subsection (5)
 (Scottish rate to be a whole number or half a whole number)—
for “The” substitute “A”, and
at the end insert “, or zero”.
In the title for “rate” substitute “rates”.
Other provisions of Part 4A of the Scotland Act 1998 are amended as follows.
In section 80A(1)(a)
 (overview of Part 4A) after “rate” insert “or rates”.
Section 80G (supplemental powers to modify enactments) is amended as follows.
In subsection (1)
 (power to modify section 11A of the Income Tax Act 2007)—
in the words before paragraph (a) for “the Scottish basic, higher and additional” substitute “Scottish”, and
in paragraph (a) for “the rates provided for under the section” substitute “Scottish rates”.
In subsection (1A)
 (power to modify references to certain rates of income tax in relation to Scottish taxpayers) for the words after “enactment” substitute “so as to make any provision that they consider necessary or expedient in consequence of or in connection with—
the powers of the Parliament under section 80C;
a Scottish rate resolution”.
In subsection (1B)
 (power to postpone effect of Scottish rate etc in relation to PAYE regulations) for paragraphs (a) and (b) substitute—
provision made by a Scottish rate resolution for a tax year,
the absence of particular provision in a Scottish rate resolution for a tax year, or
the absence of a Scottish rate resolution for a tax year,
In section 80HA(3)(b)
 (report by the Comptroller and Auditor General: meaning of “Scottish rate provisions”) for the words from “the Scottish basic rate” to the end substitute “a Scottish rate”.
This section comes into force on such day as the Treasury may by regulations appoint.
The amendments made by this section have effect in relation to the tax year appointed by the Treasury by regulations and subsequent tax years.
The tax year appointed under subsection (15) must begin on or after the day appointed under subsection (14).
Regulations under this section must be made by statutory instrument.
heading: Section 13
